tasks_completed: Cycle 1: implement rebase-conflict escalate — capture conflicted paths + main HEAD in daemon.sh before rebase --abort; add emit_rebase_conflict_escalate() to actions.py with chaining support; wire CLI dispatch; update notify string
Cycle 2: write test for rebase-conflict escalate path — assert escalate.json written with expected fields, awaiting_review[] has kind=rebase-blocked
learnings: CONFLICTED_PATHS captured via 'git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U' before rebase --abort; passed to Python as a single newline-separated arg (extra[1]) which splitlines() parses correctly.
emit-rebase-conflict-escalate added to BRIEF_ACTIONS so it gets the brief_id/project_dir positional parsing.
Chaining: if escalate.json already exists, appends to chained_failures[] rather than clobbering — important for multi-tenant hackathon scenarios where queen may have already written an escalate.
Unit-testing awaiting_review[] kind requires the state projector path (append_event → project_running_json) — running.json is derived, not written directly.
15 new tests added in test_rebase_conflict_escalate.py; 69 total pass.
